channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Il modello cloud alla prova dell’IA: è l’edge il nuovo trend
item.description: Il rapporto dei CIO col cloud non è esattamente una love story, ma è chiaro che il sodalizio è destinato a rafforzarsi: secondo IDC [in inglese] il cloud pubblico arriverà a rappresentare oltre il 70% della spesa per le nuove applicazioni software enterprise nel 2028.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Redbox owner’s bankruptcy filing is the final nail in the coffin for DVDs
item.description: Those red DVD kiosks peppering Walgreens across the country may have reached their end. Last week, media company Chicken Soup for the Soul Entertainment, which owns Redbox, filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: California considers unique safety regulations for AI companies, but faces tech firm opposition
item.description: California lawmakers are weighing a bill that would regulate powerful artificial intelligence systems.
item.dc:creator: Trân Nguyễn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Philips Hue's new powerful smart lights can illuminate every nook and cranny in your home
item.description: Housing powerful bulbs, each of the six Pillar and Runner spotlights emit bright light and can be controlled via Alexa.
item.dc:creator: cesartechradar@gmail.com (Cesar Cadenas) , TechRadar

item.title: Apple Redefines Legacy iPhone X, Original AirPods as 'Vintage' Technology
item.description: As Apple's technology evolves, the iconic iPhone X and original AirPods have reached a new phase of their lifecycle. The company has reclassified these products as 'vintage', marking a step towards obsolescence.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: EU Regulators Accuse Meta of Breaching Digital Competition Rules with Ad-Free Option
item.description: The European Union's digital competition regulators have accused social media company Meta Platforms of violating new regulations by forcing Facebook and Instagram users to choose between seeing ads or paying a fee to avoid them, in a move that could have significant implications for the tech industry.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Transform Team Planning with People-Centric Strategies
item.description: Efficient team planning requires a shift from traditional approaches. Learn how people-centric strategies can lead to better outcomes and reduced costs.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Galaxy Watch 7 May Introduce Life-Saving Features, Including Heart Attack and Stroke Detection
item.description: Samsung's upcoming Galaxy Watch 7 may feature a life-saving alert system that warns users of potential heart attacks, strokes, and other health risks, in addition to introducing health data sharing for families
item.dc:creator: cesartechradar@gmail.com (Cesar Cadenas) , TechRadar

item.title: NYT Connections today — hints and answers for Tuesday, July 2 (game #387)
item.description: Looking for NYT Connections answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, plus my commentary on the puzzles.
item.dc:creator: marc.mclaren@futurenet.com (Marc McLaren) , TechRadar

item.title: Despite adoption hurdles, healthcare is all-in on Generative AI
item.description: Generative AI (GenAI) is having a renaissance, but few industries are experiencing this like healthcare. As early adopters, everything from hospital operations and administrative duties, to clinical trials and drug discovery are being impacted by the technology.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: OpenSSH vulnerability regreSSHion puts millions of servers at risk
item.description: Researchers have uncovered a serious remote code execution vulnerability in the Open Secure Shell (OpenSSH) server that could let unauthenticated attackers obtain a root shell on servers and take them over.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: Error in new lung transplant algorithm harmed sick and dying patients
item.description: A flawed new algorithm for distributing lungs to transplant patients wound up putting people with Type O blood at a disadvantage.
item.dc:creator: Gregory Royal Pratt, The Mercury News

item.title: Eclipse Theia IDE arrives for desktop, browser
item.description: The Eclipse Foundation has announced the release of the Eclipse Theia IDE. Based on the Theia cloud and desktop IDE framework implemented in TypeScript, the Theia IDE is avialable for Windows, Linux, and macOS and can be tried online.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 10 voice scams to watch out for – and your quick action plan
item.description: Any potential scammer can copy a voice with cheap, accurate AI software in their bid to swindle you out of big money. Here's how to protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Evolving hybrid cloud strategies in modern agencies
item.description: How are the CDC and TSA managing cloud adoption to meet their missions? The post Evolving hybrid cloud strategies in modern agencies first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: EU Deems Meta's 'Pay or Consent' Ad Model Non-Compliant with Digital Markets Act
item.description: The European Union has released preliminary findings that Meta's advertising model for EU users violates the bloc's Digital Markets Act, posing potential consequences for the tech giant.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: The technology powering Taylor Swift, Netflix and the Sphere
item.description: Disguise is a pioneer in an exploding market for visual graphics in demand from Hollywood to the NBA.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Nutanix hunts disgruntled VMware customers
item.description: The new Nutanix is virtually unrecognizable from the company that created the hyperconverged infrastructure (HCI) market in 2011.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The Supreme Court keeps on hold efforts in Texas and Florida to regulate social media platforms
item.description: The Florida and Texas laws were signed by Republican governors in the months following decisions by Facebook and Twitter, now X, to cut then-President Donald Trump off over his posts related to the Jan. 6, 2021, attack on the U.S. Capitol by his supporters.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Supreme Court Avoids Review of Texas and Florida Social Media Laws Amid Contrasting Appeals Court Rulings
item.description: The Supreme Court's decision not to review the social media laws in Texas and Florida comes after federal appeals courts reached opposite conclusions, leaving the regulations in place.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court punts on Texas, Florida social media laws
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday wiped lower court rulings regarding two state laws aimed at barring social media companies from banning users based on their political views, kicking the major First Amendment challenge down the road.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, The Hill

item.title: DoD’s Joint Staff GenAI sprint lays out 8 internal use cases
item.description: Lt. Gen. Todd Isaacson, the CIO for the Joint Chiefs of Staff/J-6, said a review team is completing recommendations for leadership around using GenAI. The post DoD’s Joint Staff GenAI sprint lays out 8 internal use cases first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: How to trace and block anonymous calls
item.description: Do you want to prevent scam calls? Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses how to block hidden IDs on your phone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: European Union accuses Facebook owner Meta of breaking digital rules with paid ad-free option
item.description: European Union regulators have accused social media company Meta Platforms of breaching the bloc’s new digital competition rulebook by forcing Facebook and Instagram users to choose between seeing ads or paying to avoid them.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Autonomous trash-gobbling robot boat wages war on waterway waste
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson provides the details on the Class 3 Clearbot: an autonomous vessel for efficient marine trash/plant removal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why (and perhaps how) Apple Intelligence will make money
item.description: We’ll leave it to future retrospectives to show us if it turns out be a good or a bad thing, but artificial intelligence will change everything, so of course Apple is building a business around it. That shouldn’t surprise anyone.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 7 steps to improve analytics for data-driven organizations
item.description: When leaders say they want to be a data-driven organization, a key objective is empowering business people to use data, predictive models, generative AI capabilities, and data visualizations to improve decision-making.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Instagram creators could soon make AI versions of themselves
item.description: Think you've seen them? Meta said “AI” will appear in front of the creator’s name followed by a “beta” tag after.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Will China try to make a land grab on the moon?
item.description: “A lot of their so-called civilian space program is a military program. And I think, in effect, we are in a race,” says NASA Administrator Bill Nelson.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Cape Cod Stranding: Dolphin Rescuers Work to Deter 50+ Stranded Dolphins from Shallow Waters
item.description: Following the mass stranding of 125 dolphins on Cape Cod, rescuers are working to keep over 50 stranded creatures away from shallow waters to prevent further harm
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Too many passwords to remember? Here's what you can do
item.description: According to some unscientific studies, the average person has hundreds of passwords. That's a lot to keep track of.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: As AI gains a workplace foothold, states are trying to make sure workers don't get left behind
item.description: HARTFORD, Conn. (AP) — With many jobs expected to eventually rely on generative artificial intelligence, states are trying to help workers beef up their tech skills before they become outdated and get outfoxed by machines that are becoming increasingly smarter.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN HAIGH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Amazon is reviewing whether Perplexity AI improperly scraped online content
item.description: Amazon is reviewing claims that the artificial intelligence startup Perplexity AI is scraping content — including from prominent news sites — without approval.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Navigating the future of healthcare: Transformation, measurement and integration
item.description: How will healthcare continue to transform? The post Navigating the future of healthcare: Transformation, measurement and integration first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: The Supreme Court rules for Biden administration in a social media dispute with conservative states
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Wednesday sided with the Biden administration in a dispute with Republican-led states over how far the federal government can go to combat controversial social media posts on topics including COVID-19 and election security.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: A Vatican Radio territory in north Rome once blamed for electro-smog will become a solar farm
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis decreed Wednesday that an area of northern Rome, long the source of controversy because of electromagnetic waves emitted by Vatican Radio towers there, will now house a field of solar panels to fuel Vatican City. Citing the Vatican’s pledge in U.N.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Where tech, politics & giving meet: CEO Nicole Taylor considers Silicon Valley's busy intersection
item.description: Nicole Taylor has an insider's view of philanthropic trends from her seat as the president and CEO of the Silicon Valley Community Foundation.
item.dc:creator: THALIA BEATY, Associated Press, KRON4
